Manifest turns any webpage into a structured JSON map of what an AI agent can click, fill, and submit. One API call, no fragile selectors. Every action comes with resolved CSS/role locators, plus a requires field that encodes dependencies between elements (e.g. "select a plan before this button is clickable"). That's context aria snapshots don't give you. Python SDK, LangChain support, and an MCP server included. Built for anyone shipping browser agents.

Browser Automation

LLM Developer Tools

Manifest turns any webpage into a structured JSON map of what an AI agent can click, fill, and submit. One API call, no fragile selectors. Every action comes with resolved CSS/role locators, plus a requires field that encodes dependencies between elements (e.g. "select a plan before this button is clickable"). That's context aria snapshots don't give you. Python SDK, LangChain support, and an MCP server included. Built for anyone shipping browser agents.

 Overview
 Reviews
 Alternatives
 Built with
 Team
 More

Free Options [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 I'm Max, solo founder of Manifest (Omfang AB). I built this because every time I tried to get an AI agent to reliably interact with a webpage, I ended up hand-writing selectors that broke the moment a site's DOM changed. Browser automation tools solve "give me a browser," and content extraction tools solve "give me the content" — but nothing solved "tell me what's clickable, fillable, and submittable, and how those actions depend on each other." That's what Manifest does: one API call returns a structured JSON action manifest for any webpage, with resolved locators and a requires field that encodes cross-action dependencies (e.g. this submit button requires that field to be filled first). It's live now as REST API, Python SDK, LangChain integration, and an MCP server. [...] API, Python SDK, LangChain integration, and an MCP server. You can try it with no signup here: demo.manifest.omfang.io/demo I'm building this solo and pre-revenue, so I'd genuinely love feedback, especially from anyone building browser agents who's hit the same selector-fragility problem. What would make this useful for what you're working on?

results.title: WebMCP: Turning web pages into tools for AI agents
results.content:
Our vector search feature is nifty but currently limited to a form-based web app. Under the hood, it’s just some JavaScript functions. What if we could expose those functions directly to AI agents in a natively friendly format? That’s what WebMCP enables.

## How WebMCP works

WebMCP is an emerging standard that essentially allows web pages to be turned into an equivalent of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. The W3C Web Machine Learning Community Group leads the specification, with development championed by engineers at Google and Microsoft. In even the short time since the standard’s creation, it’s already seen a number of changes as the steering group iterates on practical, workable solutions. [...] jsx

``` [...] ```

Shows buyers where to validate your product, pricing, and reputation.

Plain HTML. No signup, script, or maintenance required.

Manifest is an AI agent observability tool focused on cost transparency for OpenClaw agents. It provides real-time token and cost tracking across agents and actions, helping teams identify expensive tasks and runaway context before bills spike. Manifest emphasizes privacy by collecting telemetry metadata rather than prompts/messages, and it supports self-hosted deployment so data can remain inside your network. It also includes notifications and thresholds to alert you when an agent approaches or exceeds spending limits.
